records.ety: [F. glu, L. glus, akin to gluten, from gluere to draw together. Cf. Gluten.]

defs: A hard brittle brownish gelatin, obtained by boiling to a jelly the skins, hoofs, etc., of animals. When gently heated with water, it becomes viscid and tenaceous, and is used as a cement for uniting substances. The name is also given to other adhesive or viscous substances.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Glued (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Gluing.]

defs: To join with glue or a viscous substance; to cause to stick or hold fast, as if with glue; to fix or fasten.
quotes: This cold, congealed blood That glues my lips, and will not let me speak.
